lib.evaluate_model¶

Classes

EvaluateModel(device, trained_model, ...)

class lib.evaluate_model.EvaluateModel(device, trained_model, testing_data)[source]¶

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • trained_model (torch.nn.Module)

  • testing_data (torch.utils.data.DataLoader)

__init__(device, trained_model, testing_data)[source]¶

Breaks out important dictonaries, takes in the trained model, creates a logger object, and creates parameters to store evaluation metrics.

Parameters:

  • cfg (dict) – Dictionary containing important information for training including

  • attributes (dict) – Dictionary containing the names of the metadata contained in the h5 image files

  • trained_model (nn.Module) – Trained model taken from the training class.

  • testing_data (DataLoader) – Data that was set aside for testing/evaluating hitfinder

Return type:

None

get_classification_report()[source]¶

Returns the classification report for the model.

Return type:

dict

get_confusion_matrix()[source]¶

Returns the confusion matrix of the testing set as a numpy array.

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

make_classification_report()[source]¶

Creates a classification report for the model and prints it.

Return type:

None

plot_confusion_matrix(path=None)[source]¶

Plots the confusion matrix of the testing set. The values in this matrix are done so that the rows total to 1.

Parameters:

path (str) – Path to where confusion matrix should be saved

Return type:

None

plot_roc_curve(path=None)[source]¶

Plots the ROC (Reciever Operating Characteristic) Curve of the testing set. The x-axis is the false positive rate and the y-axis is the true positive rate :param path: Path to where ROC curve should be saved :type path: str

Parameters:

path (str)

Return type:

None

run_testing_set()[source]¶

Runs the trained model in evaluation mode, by creating arrays of labels and predictions to compare against each other for metrics.

Return type:

None
